result.summary: Ramp exposes a public website OpenAPI surface for marketing lead submission plus developer-facing APIs, CLI, and MCP tooling documented on its developer sites, but only the website OpenAPI surface is machine-described in this corpus and its auth for developer surfaces is not determinable here.

surfaces.name: Ramp.com public website API

requiredHeaders.name: referrer

source.value: pathname of the page that made the request

requiredHeaders.description: The OpenAPI operation descriptions say to send the referrer header with the pathname of the page that made the request.

surfaces.notes: The OpenAPI document describes two email-submission endpoints under `/high-intent-prequal` and `/low-intent-no-prequal`. It includes 401/403 response shapes but does not define any security schemes, and `ramplify_app_id` is a request field rather than a user-minted credential.

surfaces.name: Ramp Developer API
surfaces.notes: Ramp's docs shell explicitly points developers to a machine-readable OpenAPI spec at `/openapi/developer-api.json`, and Ramp's llms index says to use the Developer API for server-to-server and long-lived deterministic workflows including Custom Records and Webhooks. This corpus does not include the spec contents or auth details.

surfaces.name: Ramp CLI
surfaces.notes: Ramp's llms index states that Ramp CLI is available at `https://docs.ramp.com/developer-api/v1/cli` and is preferred for agents that can run shell commands, but this corpus does not include the CLI page contents, install command, package name, or login/auth flow.

surfaces.name: Ramp MCP
surfaces.notes: Ramp's llms index says Ramp ships an MCP server for Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible assistants, but this corpus does not provide the MCP connect endpoint, transport, or authentication details.
